headline: Cannes Curveball: Jacob Elordi's Festival Dreams Hobbled by Injury
lede: Actor Jacob Elordi faces unexpected setback as a broken foot threatens his high-profile jury appearance at the Cannes Film Festival.
body:
Hollywood's rising star Jacob Elordi will not be making his anticipated Cannes Film Festival jury debut as planned, after sustaining a significant foot injury that derails his festival commitments.

The 'Euphoria' breakout and recent 'Frankenstein' lead was poised to bring youthful energy to the prestigious competition jury. Instead, Elordi will now be sidelined, watching the glamorous proceedings from afar while recovering from his unexpected medical complication.

Elordi's packed professional calendar — which recently included critically discussed turns in 'Wuthering Heights' and the latest 'Euphoria' season — suddenly encounters an unscripted pause. The injury arrives at a critical moment for the actor, whose international profile continues ascending rapidly through diverse dramatic roles.

Film festival circuits are notoriously unpredictable, and Elordi's absence represents a surprising twist in what was expected to be a breakthrough moment for the young actor. Festival organizers will likely need to quickly recalibrate their jury composition in his absence.

While specific medical details remain limited, the foot injury appears significant enough to completely preclude Elordi's participation in the upcoming Cannes proceedings.
key_facts: Elordi was set to serve on Cannes Film Festival jury
